title: Freshness + chat-extraction triggers (wire the activation layer)
description:
Make the designed triggers actually fire. Today only SessionStart (standing stale count) + the comments UserPromptSubmit hook are wired; chat extraction is manual (kb catchup); staleness surfacing repeats a standing count; no post-commit/PreCompact/SessionEnd.

Build (all stdlib, propose-not-apply, soft-signal, never blocking):
- Delta/dedupe 'surfaced' cursor so staleness is announced once on fresh→stale transition (not a standing count).
- Post-commit git hook: cheap staleness check scoped to the commit diff + from-code re-synth proposals; soft, exits 0.
- Chat triggers: PreCompact (inject extract-before-compaction directive, agent mines live context), SessionEnd (clean-ending capture), and wire chat catch-up into SessionStart reconciliation (currently only staleness reconciles). Git-independent so zero-diff sessions are caught; post-commit diff is only grounding when a commit coincides.
- kb verify <page> helper to bump verified_against (+Timeline) → clears a re-read page in one command.

Heavily kb.py/cmd_init-centric → serial. Design context: docs/ideas/repo-wiki.md, references/{intake,activation,extraction}.md, scripts/kb.py.
closed_reason: Completed: activation layer wired. Surfaced-cursor delta (announce once); post-commit hook (diff-scoped, soft, exit 0); chat triggers (PreCompact directive + SessionEnd + SessionStart catch-up w/ recall text-parse excluding subagents); self-heal bootstrap (recreates non-inheritable git hook on clone); kb verify; onboarding docs. 5 ticks. Final review: 1 blocker (cmd_verify malformed-frontmatter data loss) + 5 should-fix/nits — all fixed & verified. Mid-run defect (recall --json) caught+fixed. Dropped pytest-only test (zero-dep) — stdlib unittest is a follow-up.
